questions.q: What is the meaning of admission of a partner in a partnership firm?
questions.answer: Admission of a partner means the entry of a new partner in the existing partnership firm. The new partner brings in additional capital and shares the profits and losses of the firm.
questions.topic: Introduction to Admission of a Partner

questions.q: State the purpose of revaluing assets and liabilities at the time of admission of a partner.
questions.answer: The purpose of revaluing assets and liabilities at the time of admission of a partner is to assess the true value of the firm's assets and liabilities and to adjust the capital accounts of the partners.
key_points: True value of assets and liabilities

questions.q: What is the effect of admission of a partner on the existing partnership deed?
questions.answer: The admission of a partner leads to a change in the existing partnership deed, and a new partnership deed is required to be prepared to include the new partner.

questions.q: Define goodwill and state its treatment at the time of admission of a partner.
questions.answer: Goodwill is the reputation and prestige of a firm that enables it to earn more profits than its competitors. At the time of admission of a partner, goodwill is valued and adjusted in the capital accounts of the partners.

questions.q: What is the journal entry for the payment of premium for goodwill by the new partner?
questions.answer: The journal entry for the payment of premium for goodwill by the new partner is: Bank A/c Dr., To Premium for Goodwill A/c.

questions.q: Explain the concept of revaluation of assets and liabilities at the time of admission of a partner. Give two examples.
questions.answer: Revaluation of assets and liabilities at the time of admission of a partner means assessing the true value of the firm's assets and liabilities. Examples: (i) Increase in the value of land and building, (ii) Decrease in the value of machinery.

questions.q: Discuss the treatment of goodwill at the time of admission of a partner. State the journal entry for the valuation of goodwill.
questions.answer: Goodwill is valued at the time of admission of a partner and adjusted in the capital accounts of the partners. The journal entry for the valuation of goodwill is: Goodwill A/c Dr., To Old Partners' Capital A/c.

questions.q: Explain the concept of sacrifice ratio and state its importance at the time of admission of a partner.
questions.answer: Sacrifice ratio is the ratio in which the old partners sacrifice their share of profits in favor of the new partner. It is important as it determines the share of profits of the new partner.

questions.q: State the journal entries for the admission of a partner when the new partner brings in cash and assets.
questions.answer: The journal entries for the admission of a partner when the new partner brings in cash and assets are: (i) Bank A/c Dr., To Cash A/c, (ii) Asset A/c Dr., To New Partner's Capital A/c.

questions.q: Discuss the effect of admission of a partner on the existing partnership firm. State the changes that take place in the partnership deed.
questions.answer: The admission of a partner leads to a change in the existing partnership deed, and a new partnership deed is required to be prepared to include the new partner. The changes that take place in the partnership deed include the addition of the new partner's name, share of profits, and other terms and conditions.

questions.q: Explain the concept of reconstitution of partnership firm at the time of admission of a partner. Discuss the accounting treatment of various items such as goodwill, revaluation of assets and liabilities, and adjustment of capital accounts.
questions.answer: Reconstitution of partnership firm at the time of admission of a partner involves the accounting treatment of various items such as goodwill, revaluation of assets and liabilities, and adjustment of capital accounts. Goodwill is valued and adjusted in the capital accounts of the partners. Assets and liabilities are revalued to their true values, and the capital accounts are adjusted accordingly.

questions.q: Discuss the importance of sacrifice ratio at the time of admission of a partner. Explain the concept of goodwill and its treatment at the time of admission of a partner.
questions.answer: Sacrifice ratio is important as it determines the share of profits of the new partner. Goodwill is the reputation and prestige of a firm that enables it to earn more profits than its competitors. At the time of admission of a partner, goodwill is valued and adjusted in the capital accounts of the partners.

questions.q: Explain the concept of admission of a partner and discuss the journal entries for the admission of a partner when the new partner brings in cash, assets, and premium for goodwill.
questions.answer: Admission of a partner means the entry of a new partner in the existing partnership firm. The journal entries for the admission of a partner when the new partner brings in cash, assets, and premium for goodwill are: (i) Bank A/c Dr., To Cash A/c, (ii) Asset A/c Dr., To New Partner's Capital A/c, (iii) Premium for Goodwill A/c Dr., To New Partner's Capital A/c.

questions.q: Discuss the effect of admission of a partner on the existing partnership firm and the changes that take place in the partnership deed. Explain the concept of revaluation of assets and liabilities and its importance at the time of admission of a partner.
questions.answer: The admission of a partner leads to a change in the existing partnership deed, and a new partnership deed is required to be prepared to include the new partner. Revaluation of assets and liabilities is important as it assesses the true value of the firm's assets and liabilities and adjusts the capital accounts of the partners.

questions.q: Explain the concept of goodwill and its treatment at the time of admission of a partner. Discuss the journal entries for the valuation of goodwill and the adjustment of capital accounts.
questions.answer: Goodwill is the reputation and prestige of a firm that enables it to earn more profits than its competitors. At the time of admission of a partner, goodwill is valued and adjusted in the capital accounts of the partners. The journal entry for the valuation of goodwill is: Goodwill A/c Dr., To Old Partners' Capital A/c.
